title: centaurium pulchellum subsp. pulchellum
intro: Centaurium pulchellum subsp. pulchellum, also known as the common centaury or pink grass of Paros, is a plant species in the gentian family that is native to Europe and the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of centaurium pulchellum subsp. pulchellum include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and improve immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for digestive problems and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in combination with other herbs in herbal preparations to enhance its medicinal properties.
